items.title: S&P 500 slips 0.14% to 7,498.96, Dow closes at 52,218.58
items.content: The broad market index dropped 0.14% to end at 7,498.96, while the Nasdaq Composite slipped 0.57% to 25,690.90. The Dow Jones Industrial Average lost 6.06 points, or 0.01%, and closed at 52,218.58.

items.title: Major Indexes Close Lower as Stocks Fail to Sustain Early Gains; Oil Rises as Iran War Intensifies
items.content: US major indexes closed lower Monday as early gains faded. Oil prices rose on escalating Iran conflict. Dow had previously crossed 53,000 for the first time on July 9.

items.title: PHLX Semiconductor Index (SOX) Jumps Over 5% to 12,350
items.content: US stocks remained buoyant into key earnings (GOOGL, TSLA, INTC, IBM). The SOX jumped over 5% to 12,350 after falling over 10% last week. AI-related plays rebounded sharply.

items.title: Hang Seng Index Jumps Over 2% as State Buying Lifts Hong Kong Stocks
items.content: Hang Seng Index rose 2.36% to 25,143, gaining nearly 600 points to reclaim the 25,000 level and its 50-day moving average. Rally followed ~¥60 billion in purchases by Chinese state-backed investment platforms.

items.title: Hang Seng Opens Lower as Technology Shares Decline
items.content: Hong Kong equities began Wednesday's session softer, with HSI falling 153 points or 0.61% to 24,978 at the open. Technology shares led the decline.

items.title: US Inflation Expected to Ease to 3.8% in June 2026
items.content: Annual inflation rate in the US expected to ease to 3.8% in June 2026 from 4.2% in May, marking first slowdown in five months. CPI forecast to edge down 0.1% monthly, first decline since May 2020.

items.title: US PCE Nowcast – Past the Oil-Shock Peak
items.content: Headline PCE inflation projected to decelerate to 3.7% YoY in June, influenced by lower energy prices. Core goods inflation remains elevated due to tech demand.

items.title: Bank of America CEO Warns Stubborn Inflation Could Trigger More Fed Rate Hikes
items.content: BofA CEO Brian Moynihan warns persistent inflation may force Fed into more rate hikes. Fed's SEP expects 2026 headline PCE of 3.6% and core PCE of 3.3% (median).

items.title: Trump Declares Iran Ceasefire 'Over'
items.content: President Trump says he believes the ceasefire with Iran is over. This follows Iran allegedly striking more targets in the Strait of Hormuz after the MOU was previously declared over on July 8.

items.title: Chinese Stocks Advance on AI, Chip Rally; Hong Kong Slips
items.content: Chinese mainland stocks advanced, led by gains in AI, semiconductor and metal shares. Hong Kong equities declined as investors booked profits after the previous state-buying rally.

items.title: China AI Chip Stocks Rally After Z.AI's Domestic-Chip Data Center Milestone
items.content: Chinese AI and semiconductor stocks rallied after Bloomberg reported AI startup Z.AI completed a large data center powered entirely by domestically produced chips, reinforcing investor confidence in China's chip self-sufficiency push.

items.title: Q2 Earnings Roll In: GOOGL, TSLA, IBM, INTC Results; AMC Soars 26%
items.content: Second quarter earnings continue. GOOGL -1.24%, TSLA -1.30%, IBM -2.25%, INTC -2.68% on the day. AMC surged 26% on 'superb' Q2 results. Domino's also rose post-earnings.
